summary: A flawless matrix blend captures an incendiary 1982 performance – Jerry’s ripping guitar and a second‑set packed with Stagger Lee, China > Rider and other classics.
review: The matrix mix of audience and soundboard sources is praised as seamless and crystal‑clear, delivering every instrument with excellent balance. The band is described as “on top of the music” all night, with Jerry’s voice and solos, Brent’s keyboards, Phil’s bass fury and Bill & Mickey’s driving drums highlighted throughout a wildly energetic set.

ai_rating.rationale: All but one reviewer gave 5‑star ratings, extolling both the musical performance and the matrix recording; the lone negative comment is outweighed by overwhelming praise.

recording_quality.technical_notes: The audience (AUD) and soundboard (SBD) tracks are blended flawlessly, providing crisp clarity and a full‑stage feel. One reviewer noted a slight speed mismatch between the sources, but most consider it corrected and superb.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers highlight a smooth, flowing set‑list with an especially epic second set; transitions (e.g., China into Rider) are called seamless and the overall pacing is described as “smoking” from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for powerful vocals, patient phrasing, soaring guitar tone, and marathon solos throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as “raging,” present in every song, and even thanked the audience before the encore; his bass drives the band forward.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for memorable slide work, heartfelt banter, and a vocal performance that stands out on several numbers.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s electric‑piano work and B3 organ solos are called outstanding; his vocals on “Friend of the Devil” receive special acclaim.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart are lauded as amazing, providing relentless drive and dynamic support to every song.
